helpful: Provisioned petition.txt and questions-presented.txt — the QP section and the docket's CFR/waiver/amici entries carried most of the signal
statpack per-fee-class grant rates (paid vs IFP) — the right anchor for a paid petition
statpack relist/CVSG cuts for calibrating what a pre-conference docket can and cannot show
gaps: CourtListener MCP daily quota (1200/day) was already exhausted at cell start; every search but one returned HTTP 429, and the one success showed the docket index barely covers SCOTUS anyway — a per-cell quota reservation or a supremecourt.gov-docket search surface would help
No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions — the strongest observable pre-conference grant signal on this docket had to be weighted from general knowledge rather than corpus data
The brief-in-opposition did not exist yet (response due 2026-08-26); a re-predict trigger after BIO filing / first conference would let the relist signal do its work
notes: Forward cell predicted from provisioned inputs + corpus base rates. Two fedcourts query calls (~90 MB ranged reads total) were enough; the CourtListener outage degraded but did not block the cell.
